contents.value:
```markdown
# H1 - Document title
## H2 - Section
### H3 - Subsection
#### H4
##### H5
###### H6
```
**Headings** create document structure. Use only one H1 per document. Leave a blank line before and after headings.

```markdown
*italic* or _italic_
**bold** or __bold__
***bold italic***
~~strikethrough~~
```
**Text formatting** in Markdown. Asterisks and underscores for emphasis, tildes for strikethrough (GFM).

```markdown
[Click here](https://example.com)
[With title](https://example.com "Title")
[Reference][id]

[id]: https://example.com
```
**Links** can be inline, reference-style, or autolinked. URLs in angle brackets are autolinked.

```markdown
- Unordered item
  - Nested item

1. Ordered item
2. Second item

- [ ] Task incomplete
- [x] Task complete
```
**Lists** - Use `-`, `*`, or `+` for unordered. Numbers for ordered. `[ ]`/`[x]` for tasks.

```markdown
| Name  | Age | City    |
|:------|:---:|--------:|
| Alice |  30 | NYC     |
| Bob   |  25 | London  |
```
**Tables** use pipes `|` and hyphens `-`. Colon placement controls alignment: `:---` left, `:---:` center, `---:` right.

```markdown
> This is a quote
> 
> > Nested quote
```
**Blockquotes** prefix lines with `>`. Can contain other Markdown elements. Useful for callouts and citations.

```markdown
![Alt text](image.png)
![Logo](https://example.com/logo.png "Title")
```
**Images** use `![alt](url)` syntax. Alt text is important for accessibility. Title appears on hover.

```markdown
---
title: My Post
date: 2024-01-01
tags: [tutorial, guide]
draft: false
---
```
**Frontmatter** is YAML metadata between `---` delimiters. Used by Jekyll, Hugo, Gatsby, and other static site generators.
